title: BUG: Remove method setting in summary2 of genmod
body:
- [X] closes #9315 
- [X] tests added / passed. 
- [X] code/documentation is well formatted.  
- [X] properly formatted commit message. See 
      [NumPy's guide](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy-1.15.1/dev/gitwash/development_workflow.html#writing-the-commit-message). 

<details>


**Notes**:

* It is essential that you add a test when making code changes. Tests are not 
  needed for doc changes.
* When adding a new function, test values should usually be verified in another package (e.g., R/SAS/Stata).
* When fixing a bug, you must add a test that would produce the bug in main and
  then show that it is fixed with the new code.
* New code additions must be well formatted. Changes should pass flake8. If on Linux or OSX, you can
  verify your changes are well formatted by running 
  ```
  git diff upstream/main -u -- "*.py" | flake8 --diff --isolated
  ```
  assuming `flake8` is installed. This command is also available on Windows 
  using the Windows System for Linux once `flake8` is installed in the 
  local Linux environment. While passing this test is not required, it is good practice and it help 
  improve code quality in `statsmodels`.
* Docstring additions must render correctly, including escapes and LaTeX.

</details>

step_plan.summary: Remove the line in GLMResults.summary2 that unconditionally sets self.method = 'IRLS' so the summary2 output uses the method attribute already set by the fit routines. Run the relevant tests (including a new test that reproduces the bug) to verify the fix and ensure no regressions.

steps.operation: Remove unconditional assignment of self.method = 'IRLS' in the GLMResults.summary2 method
steps.reason: GLMResults.summary2 currently overwrites the results.method attribute with the literal 'IRLS' before summary construction. The method attribute is already set correctly by the fit routines (_fit_irls sets 'IRLS', _fit_gradient sets the optimizer name). Removing this assignment ensures summary2 displays the actual method used during fitting.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: summary2 output will now reflect the method recorded by the fit routines (e.g., 'newton', other optimizer names) instead of always showing 'IRLS'. Tests: Requires running and keeping the added unit test that fits a GLM with a non-IRLS optimizer and asserts summary2 reports that optimizer; run the genmod test suite and iolib/summary2 tests to ensure no regressions. Dependents: Any downstream code or tests that (incorrectly) relied on summary2 to set results.method to 'IRLS' will observe the original method value instead; this is a behavioral correction rather than an API change. Documentation/API: No public API changes beyond corrected summary output; consumers parsing summary text should be unaffected except they will see the correct method string. Performance: None. Risk: Low, but validate with the new and existing tests to ensure summary formatting still constructs the 'Method:' entry from results.method as expected.
